zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne Russell於1482年出生在英國素可貝維其可時，她的父親James Russell Gentleman of the Privy Chamber, Baron Russell, of Cheyneys27歲，母親Alicia Wyse23歲。她於大約1495年與John Napier在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1550年享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne Russell was born in 1482, in Berwick, Sussex, England, her father, James Russell Gentleman of the Privy Chamber, Baron Russell, of Cheyneys, was 27 and her mother, Alicia Wyse, was 23. She married John Napier about 1495, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1550, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne Russell est née en 1482, à Berwick, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, James Russell Gentleman of the Privy Chamber, Baron Russell, of Cheyneys, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alicia Wyse, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé John Napier environ 1495, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1550, à l’âge de 68 ans.

Sie hat um 1495 in Scotland John Napier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1550 im Alter von 68 Jahren.

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Russell(이)가 1482년 영국 수세스 버윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Russell Gentleman of the Privy Chamber, Baron Russell, of Cheyneys(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alicia Wyse(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1495년 스컷랜드에서 John Napier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1550년 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
